text_blocks.text: lead to more growth and did not benefit
the poor. Much of the increased spending
has gone to current expenditures related to
wages, transfers, and subsidies. The wage
bill today constitutes over 15% of GDP
and 55% of total public spending; these
are some of the highest levels on the
African continent. The recent rapid growth
in SACU revenues has, however, reversed
the fiscal situation, and a sizeable surplus
today account for over 60% of total
government revenues. On the positive side,
the external debt burden has declined
markedly over the last 20 years, and
domestic debt is almost negligible; external
debt as a percent of GDP was less than
